commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ab707179f92ebf84dee3c5da16ba16cd522899f4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Voice Call session-key resolution, related docs/tests, plugin doctor session-store owner discovery, startup/doctor session migration, and shared session-route parsing.

PR surface: Source +915, Tests +2365, Docs +12. Total +3292 across 43 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source-level checks show current main and v2026.6.10 still return raw generated voice:* / voice:call:* keys, while the PR head changes the resolver path; I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer review is needed for the explicit-key compatibility decision and current-head real behavior proof; this is not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(voice-call): emit canonical session keys
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: voice-call generated persisted session keys still used raw `voice:*` buckets, so multi-agent voice routing could diverge from the gateway's canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` session key shape.
- Solution: generate canonical voice session keys with the normalized voice agent id while preserving explicit session key overrides.
- What changed: updated the voice-call session key resolver and refreshed manager/runtime/response-generator tests that assert generated persisted keys.
- What did NOT change: explicit session key overrides still pass through unchanged.
- Session-state upgrade boundary: existing raw generated `voice:*` and `voice:call:*` session-store entries are covered by the existing orphan-key migration path; the added proof below shows they migrate to the same canonical keys now generated by the resolver.

Fixes #83967

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — this is the existing PR #89884 for linked issue #83967, not a fan-out or follow-up to another merged PR.

## Maintainer readiness

- Fix classification: Root-cause source-of-truth session-state/data-format contract fix for generated Voice Call session keys.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for the local resolver, manager persistence, embedded-agent session key, and raw generated session-store upgrade paths; live carrier/channel proof remains explicitly untested.
- Why it matters / User impact: Without canonical generated keys, Voice Call can persist generated state under raw `voice:*` buckets while the gateway/session store expects agent-scoped `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` keys, which can reset or split per-agent voice conversation state.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: `resolveVoiceCallSessionKey()` is the source-of-truth resolver for generated Voice Call session key identity, and the canonical session-state contract is `agent:<agentId>:voice:*`; this patch fixes that contract at the resolver boundary rather than masking it downstream.
- Related open PR scan / canonical PR check: Existing PR diagnostics for #89884 re-read the linked issue, PR discussion, review findings, diff, checks, and `check-upstream-fixed`; `origin/main` has not covered #83967 and the current maintainer signal is review of this PR's generated-key cutover/upgrade proof.
- Out of scope: This does not canonicalize explicit user-provided session keys, does not change live provider transport behavior, and does not add a new migration; it proves the existing orphan-key migration covers raw generated Voice Call session-store buckets.
- Patch quality notes: The diff is limited to one source file plus focused tests; no unrelated refactor, no repo temp files, no lockfile churn, and verification is bound to the current rebased HEAD by the daily-fix verification marker.
- Target test file: `extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts`, and `extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts`.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The incorrect persisted identity originated at generated session-key resolution, so emitting canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` keys from the resolver aligns every generated manager/runtime/response-generator caller with the gateway store namespace instead of masking the mismatch after persistence.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated voice-call manager paths now create and persist canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` keys instead of bare `voice:*` keys, explicit session key overrides remain unchanged, and existing raw generated voice session-store entries upgrade to the same canonical keys.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout in `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-89884` with Node/pnpm, the voice-call resolver and manager create/persist/reload paths, and the gateway session-store migration code loaded through `tsx`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/voice-call/src/config.ts extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm format:check -- extensions/voice-call/src/config.ts extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm exec tsx inline proof script for generated resolver targets, manager create/persist/reload path, explicit override preservation, and raw generated session-store migration
```
- Evidence after fix: Console output from the local voice-call proof run:
```text
[voice-call] Inbound call accepted: policy is open
[voice-call] Created inbound call record: 7081fe33-fd29-48e3-a094-089ede756373 from +1 (555) 000-1111
[voice-call] Outbound call initiated: callId=2639a6fa-90e6-47c8-a9fc-6e6b1e9d7702 providerCallId=provider-call-2 mode=notify preConnectDtmf=no initialMessage=no
{
  "generatedResolverTargets": {
    "perPhone": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "perCall": "agent:voice:voice:call:call-123"
  },
  "managerCreatePersistReloadPath": {
    "activeInboundSessionKey": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "persistedInboundSessionKey": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "providerCallIdMappedToPersistedCall": true,
    "explicitOutboundSessionKey": "meet-room-1",
    "persistedExplicitOutboundSessionKey": "meet-room-1",
    "outboundSuccess": true
  },
  "rawGeneratedSessionUpgradePath": {
    "migrationChanges": [
      "Canonicalized 2 orphaned session key(s) in /tmp/openclaw-voice-call-proof-4Dyq8f/.openclaw/agents/voice/sessions/sessions.json"
    ],
    "migratedPhoneKey": "agent:voice:voice:15550001111",
    "migratedPhoneSessionId": "phone-session",
    "migratedCallKey": "agent:voice:voice:call:call-123",
    "migratedCallSessionId": "call-session",
    "rawKeysRemoved": true
  }
}
```
- Observed result after fix: The generated per-phone and per-call resolver targets are canonical agent-scoped keys; inbound manager state is active and persisted with the canonical key; outbound explicit `meet-room-1` is preserved; and the existing migration path canonicalizes two raw generated voice session keys while preserving their stored session ids and removing the raw `voice:*` entries.
- What was not tested: Live carrier/provider calls, Desktop, Mantis, Telegram, and other remote channel proof were not run in this local environment. The proof covers local voice-call resolver/manager/session-store behavior and discloses the live telephony boundary for maintainer revalidation.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001: Upgrade proof is supplied above for existing raw generated Voice Call sessions. The proof seeds raw generated `voice:15550001111` and `voice:call:call-123` entries, runs the existing session-store migration path, and observes canonical `agent:voice:voice:15550001111` and `agent:voice:voice:call:call-123` keys with session ids preserved.
- RF-002: The session-state risk is still disclosed. The proof shows the raw generated agent session-store entries migrate to the same canonical keys now emitted by `resolveVoiceCallSessionKey()`, while explicit overrides remain unchanged.
- RF-003: No production code repair was needed after syncing with `origin/main`; the remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of the canonical generated-key cutover plus normal merge/CI gating.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: session-state`, compatibility/data-format risk from changing generated persisted Voice Call session identity.
- Risk explanation: New generated Voice Call sessions move from raw `voice:*` / `voice:call:*` keys to canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` / `agent:<agentId>:voice:call:*` keys. That is intentional because persisted generated voice state should live in the same agent-scoped namespace as the gateway session store.
- Why acceptable: Explicit session keys still pass through unchanged, so user-selected room/session identities are not rewritten. Existing raw generated session-store buckets are covered by the already-tested orphan-key migration path, and the local proof above verifies raw generated phone/call keys migrate to the new canonical targets with session ids preserved and raw keys removed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: focused extension unit tests plus direct local manager/session-store execution.
- Target test files: `extensions/voice-call/src/config.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/outbound.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts`, `extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: generated voice session keys include normalized agent id for default, configured-agent, per-phone, and per-call paths.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the resolver owns key generation, manager/runtime/response-generator tests verify generated keys reach persisted call and embedded-agent paths, and the migration proof covers pre-existing raw generated session-store buckets.

## Verification

```bash
node scripts/test-extension.mjs voice-call
```

After resolving the `origin/main` merge conflict on 2026-06-25, `node scripts/test-extension.mjs voice-call` passed in the PR worktree with 50 test files and 460 tests. The earlier full resolver/manager/session-store proof remains the behavioral proof for the generated session-key change; this run verifies the rebased conflict resolution against the current extension test suite.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The source resolver contract for generated Voice Call session state was wrong: `resolveVoiceCallSessionKey()` generated only the `voice:*` suffix and left agent scoping to later sandbox-session wrapping, while persisted voice call/session entries still used the raw key.
- Missing detection / guardrail: config tests covered per-phone/per-call suffixes but did not assert the canonical `agent:<agentId>:voice:*` persisted-key contract or the raw generated session-store upgrade boundary.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The resolver is the generation boundary for persisted Voice Call session identity, so canonicalizing there fixes every generated caller at the source while preserving explicit override behavior.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
